content.problemStatement:
Gigi got bored and started to open/reopen some programs on his computer.

By reopening a program, we mean that the program was previously opened, wasn't closed in the meantime, and is brought into attention again.

After a while, Gigi wants to know the order in which the programs show up if he were to press `alt + tab`.

Note that the `alt + tab` function displays a list of all **unique** active programs where the **most recently opened** or **reopened** program occupies the top of the list.

Given that Gigi opened/reopened NNN programs where the name of the ithi^{th}ith program is denoted by SiS_iSi​, find the order in which the programs are displayed when pressing `alt + tab`.
constraints: 1 <= N <= 40000 (sum of N over all test cases <= 40000)
Si contains only uppercase English letters
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the number of program openings/reopenings. Then N lines follow, each containing a program name Si consisting of only uppercase English letters (2 ≤ |Si| ≤ 45).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the concatenation of the last two characters of each distinct active program, ordered from the most recently opened/reopened to the least recent.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Processing from the end, the distinct active programs are WINE, TERMINAL, GIMP, NAUTILUS, FIREFOX, GEDIT. Their last two characters are NE, AL, MP, US, OX, IT respectively; concatenated they give NEALMPUSOXIT.
The final order is IDK (most recent) then YDK. Their suffixes are DK and DK, resulting in DKDK.
Only one program exists, ABC. Its last two characters are BC.

hidden.label: tricky case (minimum length names, interleaved repeats)

solution.approach: The Alt‑Tab list contains each program at most once, with the most recent occurrence first. Scan the sequence of program names from the end, keep a hash set of names already seen, and whenever a name is not in the set, append its last two characters to the answer. This directly yields the required order. The algorithm runs in O(N) time per test case and uses O(U) extra memory, where U is the number of distinct programs.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(U) where U is the number of distinct programs in a test case (at most N).

code.Python3:
import sys
def main():
    data=sys.stdin.read().splitlines()
    it=iter(data)
    T=int(next(it))
    out=[]
    for _ in range(T):
        N=int(next(it))
        names=[next(it).strip() for _ in range(N)]
        seen=set()
        parts=[]
        for name in reversed(names):
            if name not in seen:
                seen.add(name)
                parts.append(name[-2:])
        out.append(''.join(parts))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))
if __name__=='__main__':
    main()
